info.title: VSS Video Analytics APIs
info.description: REST API endpoints in Video Analytics API microservice

tags.name: metrics
tags.description: Endpoint(s) related to computation of key performance indicators.

tags.name: livez
tags.description: Endpoint(s) used to check if server is live.

get.summary: It is used to calculate occupancy based on IN/OUT tripwire events and the value of reset occupancy. It is advised to reset a place's occupancy by using '/occupancy/reset' endpoint. This helps in keeping the occupancy calculated to be accurate.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed.

post.summary: Reset the occupancy of a place

requestBody.description: Details regarding the current occupancy of a place

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if request body doesn't follow schema.

get.summary: Get a histogram of tripwire counts. Please check the input descriptions to check its dependencies.

parameters.name: fromTimestamp
parameters.description: This parameter must be used together with toTimestamp. However, it is not compatible with minutesAgo.

parameters.name: toTimestamp
parameters.description: This parameter must be used together with fromTimestamp. However, it is not compatible with minutesAgo.

parameters.name: minutesAgo
parameters.description: This parameter is not compatible with fromTimestamp and toTimestamp.

parameters.name: bucketCount
parameters.description: This parameter provided by user is a suggestion which will be taken into account while calculating bucket size in sec (which will be a multiple of 5). There is a possibilty that the result will have less/more buckets than the one provided in the input (i.e. it can go above 1000 buckets).

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: Unique Object Count for a given Place. The data from multiple sensors are processed to estimate an approximate number of Objects / Persons in a given place

parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with place. Only one of them can exist in the query. When objectId is present, then only one sensorId can be present in the sensorIds array.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: Unique Objects, represents an Object tracked across multiple sensors, Each object comprise of list of Behavior or Tracklets. The Behavior represents an object traversal/appearance etc in a given sensor with respect to time and space. Please check the input descriptions to check its dependencies.

parameters.name: sensorIds
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with place. Only one of them can exist in the query. When objectId is present, then only one sensorId can be present in the sensorIds array.

parameters.name: place
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with sensorIds and globalId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: globalId
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with sensorIds and place. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: maxResultSize
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with globalId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: Get the last record either from RTLS or AMR(mission control) source. The record can be further filtered based on objectType that can be obtained from a particular source. Example: 'Person' objectType can be used in case of RTLS source, 'AMR' objectType can be used in case of AMR source

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: Get raw frame metadata

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with maxResultSize, timestamp or time range params (fromTimestamp and toTimestamp). Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: This parameter is used for querying frame at a particular point of time. It is incompatible with maxResultSize, frameId or time range params (fromTimestamp and toTimestamp). Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: fromTimestamp
parameters.description: This parameter is used for querying frames in a time range. It is incompatible with timestamp or frameId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: toTimestamp
parameters.description: This parameter is used for querying frames in a time range. It is incompatible with timestamp or frameId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: maxResultSize
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with timestamp or frameId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: It is used to calculate pts in milliseconds for a given frameId. It can also be used to calculate estimated pts for a given timestamp. This endpoint is used for video files (sensors) that are streamed using nvstreamer.

parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with frameId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with timestamp. Only one of them can exist in the query.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: Retrieves behavior(s) from database.

parameters.name: sensorId
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with place. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: place
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with sensorId. Only one of them can exist in the query.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: This parameter is incompatible with either 'place', 'queryString' or 'maxResultSize'. It also requires 'sensorId', 'fromTimestamp' and 'toTimestamp' in the query.

parameters.name: objectType
parameters.description: If this parameter is present in the query, then 'objectId' should also be present in the query.

parameters.name: fromTimestamp
parameters.description: If this parameter is present in the query, then 'toTimestamp' should also be present in the query.

parameters.name: toTimestamp
parameters.description: If this parameter is present in the query, then 'fromTimestamp' should also be present in the query.

parameters.name: queryString
parameters.description: 'queryString' should follow lucene syntax.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

get.summary: It is used to get the start and end pts of a behavior in milliseconds. This endpoint is used for video files (sensors) that are streamed using nvstreamer.

400.description: Bad Request. This response will be sent if any of the required params are not present or if extra params are passed. It will also be sent if parameter dependencies (mentioned in parameter descriptions) are not satisfied.

parameters.description: If this parameter is present in the query, then 'fromTimestamp' should also be present in the query.

get.summary: Get Tripwire Events. Please check the input descriptions to check its dependencies.

parameters.name: sensorId

parameters.description: This parameter must be used together with sensorId and it is incompatible with place.

parameters.description: This parameter is used to obtain effective/actual set of tripwire events. If effective events are being obtained, then only the latest tripwire event of each object will be retrieved.

Access-Control-Allow-Origin.description: CORS header to allow cross-origin requests.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: The maximum number of requests allowed in the current time period.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests remaining in the current time period.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The time when the rate limit will be reset (Unix timestamp).

type.description: Allowed values for tripwire events: 'IN', 'OUT'. Allowed values for roi events: 'ENTRY', 'EXIT'.

center.description: center is represented using WKT format of Point

count.description: Effective counts are used to count only the effective event generated by an object. An effective tripwire event can be defined with the following example: Suppose an object generates tripwire events in the following sequence: IN, OUT and IN again, then the effective count in this case considers only the final IN event. This is helpful to handle loitering behavior of an object.

actualCount.description: Actual tripwire count is the count of all the events that have occurred.

count.description: Effective counts are used to count only the effective event generated by an object. An effective tripwire event can be defined with the following example: Suppose an object generates tripwire events in the following sequence: IN, OUT and IN again, then the effective count in this case considers only the final IN event. This is helpful to handle loitering behavior of an object.

actualCount.description: Actual tripwire count is the count of all the events that have occurred.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last calibration data modification
